# STYLES.md

> A machine-readable style standard for human and AI collaboration.

STYLES.md is an open format for writing down how a project communicates —
voice, tone, design tokens, and AI agent constraints — in a single Markdown
file that both humans and language models can read, follow, and automate against.

It is part of a three-pillar governance ecosystem: ACCESSIBILITY.md (inclusion
requirements), SUSTAINABILITY.md (compute-efficiency constraints), and STYLES.md
(voice, design tokens, and AI agent style rules).

## Core standards

- [STYLES.md](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/STYLES.md): Canonical style
  implementation. Covers content standards (§ 2), design tokens and typography
  (§ 3), accessibility and semantic logic (§ 4), AI agent instructions (§ 5),
  content governance (§ 6), and references (§ 7).
- [AGENTS.md](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/AGENTS.md): Operational
  instructions for AI coding agents working in this repository.
- [ACCESSIBILITY.md](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/ACCESSIBILITY.md):
  Foundational accessibility commitments and contributor guardrails. Overrides
  STYLES.md when they conflict.
- [SUSTAINABILITY.md](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/SUSTAINABILITY.md):
  Environmental and compute-efficiency constraints. Non-negotiable.

## Templates and examples

- [STYLES-template.md](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/STYLES-template.md):
  Minimal skeleton to copy and customize for a new project.
- [examples/](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/examples/): Worked examples
  for formal-gov, tech-minimal, low-bandwidth, and navigation-menus contexts.
- [templates/brand-pack/](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/templates/brand-pack/):
  Copy-ready brand portability kit: CSS tokens, component contracts, voice rules,
  and AI enforcement instructions.
- [templates/brand-packs/](https://mgifford.github.io/STYLES.md/templates/brand-packs/):
  Multi-brand starter profiles for projects that need more than one reusable
  style system.

## Key concepts for LLMs

- **AI agent constraints live in AGENTS.md and STYLES.md § 5.** Reference both
  when constructing a system prompt for an AI coding agent.
- **Non-negotiable guardrails** are in ACCESSIBILITY.md and SUSTAINABILITY.md.
  When a rule in STYLES.md conflicts with either, the guardrail wins.
- **Design tokens** are documented in STYLES.md § 3.1 with light and dark values
  and their accessibility requirements. Token names are semantic.
- **Voice and tone rules** are in STYLES.md § 2. Plain language, active voice,
  American English by default.
- **LLM integration guidance** is in STYLES.md §§ 5–5.1. It covers how to include
  this file in context windows, the llms.txt convention, semantic token naming,
  component contracts, and decision rationale.
